reasoning: The probability of Greenland announcing an independence referendum before July 1, 2025 is extremely low. The March 2025 snap election was won by the gradualist Demokraatit party, which favors a slow, measured approach emphasizing economic development over immediate sovereignty. More importantly, the parliamentary Constitutional Commission established to evaluate the steps toward independence is not expected to present its conclusions until late 2026, and all major parties have signaled they will wait for this framework before initiating a referendum process. With only two months remaining between the knowledge cutoff (May 2025) and the resolution date (July 2025), and zero momentum for an immediate vote, an officially scheduled referendum is highly unlikely.
